title: Al Jazeera Weekly Wrap: 'Under Cover of Ceasefire, Israel Increases Grip' — 925 Movement Obstacles, 20-Year High
detail: Al Jazeera published a major weekly analysis on April 28–29, 2026, titled 'Under cover of ceasefire, Israel increases grip,' documenting how ceasefires in Gaza, Lebanon, and Iran are functioning 'less as true pauses in hostilities than as cover for accelerated fact-making on the ground.' Key findings: (1) OCHA documented 925 movement obstacles in the West Bank — the highest total in over 20 years, 43% above the two-decade average; (2) 817 Palestinians killed since the October 10, 2025 ceasefire (updated to 824+ by the UN Security Council briefing April 28); (3) Israeli forces and settlers continued pressing into Palestinian-administered areas during the ceasefire period; (4) IDF Yellow Line controls approximately 60% of Gaza; (5) Talks on Hamas disarmament and a permanent arrangement remain deadlocked with no sign of progress. Phase II negotiations under the US 'Board of Peace' framework remain stalled. Palestinian municipal elections held April 25 with 23% turnout in Gaza — contested and criticized for Hamas exclusion. The analysis concluded that the core Gaza conflict is 'far from resolution' with a prolonged stalemate as the most likely near-term outcome.

media.caption: Palestinian women at Nasser Hospital in Khan Younis after Israeli airstrike, April 2026; Al Jazeera weekly wrap documents Israel's expanding control under ceasefire cover

title: OHCHR: Palestinians Across Gaza 'Unsafe' Six Months After Ceasefire — 92% Housing Destroyed, Forcible Transfer Ongoing
detail: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk released a major statement on April 29, 2026 stating that Palestinians across Gaza remain 'unsafe' six months after the ceasefire announcement. Key OHCHR findings: (1) 92% of Gaza's housing stock destroyed or damaged — reconstruction will take decades; (2) Continuous forced displacement of civilians amounts to 'forcible transfer' under international humanitarian law; (3) 589 aid workers killed in Gaza since October 7, 2023 — including 397 UN staff — making this the deadliest conflict for aid workers in UN recorded history; (4) 294 journalists killed since October 7 — highest toll in any conflict in CPJ's 40-year history; (5) Ongoing Israeli military operations in Gaza despite the nominal ceasefire continue to cause civilian casualties and destruction; (6) The OHCHR documented that the regional conflict — Iran, Lebanon — has 'eclipsed' a surge in human rights violations against Palestinians in the West Bank, where settler violence, IDF operations, and displacement continued unabated. Türk called on all parties to uphold international humanitarian law and for accountability mechanisms to be activated.

media.caption: OHCHR statement: Gaza remains unsafe 6 months after ceasefire; 92% housing destroyed; continuous displacement amounts to forcible transfer under IHL, April 29, 2026
